page_header.label: OPEN SOURCE
page_header.title: Don't Trust, Verify
page_header.description: Every line of ZKIRA Pay is open source and auditable. Verify our claims, build from source, contribute to the future of private payments.

items.label: CORE
items.title: zkira-pay
items.description: Core Anchor programs implementing escrow, stealth addresses, and claim verification. The foundation of ZKIRA Pay's on-chain functionality.

items.label: APP
items.title: zkira-pay
items.description: Web application for creating and claiming payment links. Built with Next.js, React, and TypeScript for a seamless user experience.

items.label: SDK
items.title: @zkira/sdk
items.description: TypeScript SDK for developers to integrate ZKIRA Pay into their applications. Clean API for payment creation, monitoring, and claiming.

items.label: WIDGET
items.title: @zkira/widget
items.description: Embeddable React widget for any website. Drop-in payment buttons and forms that integrate seamlessly with existing designs.

items.label: API
items.title: zkira-api
items.description: REST API server providing HTTP endpoints for payment management, gas relaying, and metadata storage. Built with Node.js and Express.

items.label: BOT
items.title: zkira-bot
items.description: Telegram bot for creating and sharing payment links directly in chat. Non-custodial bot generates secure links without handling funds.

sections.label: BUILD VERIFICATION
sections.title: How to Verify
sections.subtitle: Don't trust our deployed programs. Build from source and verify that the on-chain bytecode matches the published code.

sections.title: terminal
sections.code:
# Clone the protocol repository
$ git clone https://github.com/zkira-pay/zkira-pay.git
$ cd zkira-pay

# Build the Anchor programs
$ anchor build

# Verify program IDs match deployed versions
$ anchor keys list

# Compare bytecode hashes
$ solana program show <program_id> --url devnet

paragraphs: Our Anchor programs are built deterministically, meaning the same source code will always produce identical bytecode. This allows anyone to verify that our deployed programs match the published source.
Same Rust toolchain version across all builds, locked dependency versions in Cargo.lock, and reproducible build environment ensure deterministic builds.
